    private void invokePostRule(PostRuleMethod prm, int solutionIndex) {
        try {
            prm.method.setAccessible(true);
            prm.method.invoke(moduleAlg);
            log.info("Executed POST rule: {} for solution {}", prm.rule.getCode(), solutionIndex);
        } catch (IllegalAccessException e) {
            throw new AlgLoaderException(
                    "Failed to access POST rule method: " + prm.method.getName(), e);
        } catch (InvocationTargetException e) {
            Throwable cause = e.getCause() != null ? e.getCause() : e;
            throw new AlgLoaderException(
                    "POST rule failed: ruleCode=" + prm.rule.getCode()
                            + ", method=" + prm.method.getName()
                            + ", solutionIndex=" + solutionIndex
                            + ", message=" + cause.getMessage(), cause);
        }
    }

    /**
     * ķÄĄŅéŻÕ╝┐ķÄĄŌé¼ķÅłÕĪÆOSTńæÖÕŗ½Õ×»ķ¬×ĶĘ║ń╝ōń╗öÕ®╗uleķł½µēóethodķÅäńŖ▓ńÜĀ
     */
    private List<PostRuleMethod> scanPostRuleMethods() {
        List<Rule> postRules = module.getAllRules(CalcStage.POST);
        if (postRules == null || postRules.isEmpty()) {
            return new ArrayList<>();
        }

        Map<String, Method> allRuleMethods = buildAllRuleMethods();
        List<PostRuleMethod> result = new ArrayList<>();

        for (Rule rule : postRules) {
            Method method = allRuleMethods.get(rule.getCode());
            if (method == null) {
                throw new AlgLoaderException(
                        "POST rule method not found for rule code: " + rule.getCode()
                                + " in class " + moduleAlg.getClass().getName());
            }
            result.add(new PostRuleMethod(rule, method));
            log.info("Scanned POST rule: {} -> {}", rule.getCode(), method.getName());
        }

        log.info("Scanned {} POST rule methods", result.size());
        return result;
    }

    private Map<String, Method> buildAllRuleMethods() {
        Method[] methods = moduleAlg.getClass().getDeclaredMethods();
        Map<String, Method> allMethods = new HashMap<>();
        for (Method method : methods) {
            allMethods.put(method.getName(), method);
        }
        Map<String, Method> ruleMethods = new LinkedHashMap<>();
        for (Rule rule : module.getAllRules()) {
            Method method = allMethods.get(rule.getCode());
            if (method != null) {
                ruleMethods.put(rule.getCode(), method);
            }
        }
        return ruleMethods;
    }

    /**
     * ķŹÉÕæ┤Õä┤ń╗½õ╝Öń┤░POSTńæÖÕŗ½Õ×»ķÅéĶ¦äńĪČńĆĄ?
     */
    private static class PostRuleMethod {

package com.jmix.scenario.ruletest;

import static org.junit.jupiter.api.Assertions.assertEquals;
import static org.junit.jupiter.api.Assertions.assertTrue;

import com.jmix.executor.southinf.ModuleAlgBase;

import com.jmix.tool.bbuilder.anno.PartAnno;

import lombok.extern.slf4j.Slf4j;

import org.junit.jupiter.api.Test;

/**
 * POSTķ¢Ėµ░¼µĄĘķÉżÕŚÖµŗŗķöøÕŗŁµÜ¼ķÉ¤µ¼ÅÕ½¼ķŹ©ŅłÜ├╣ńĆŻŅģĀµ¦Ė
 *
 * @since 2026-05-03
 */
@Slf4j
public class PostCalcRuleTest extends ModuleScenarioTestBase {

    public PostCalcRuleTest() {
        super(PostCalcConstraint.class);
    }

    @ModuleAnno(id = 123L)
    public static class PostCalcConstraint extends ModuleAlgBase {

        @PartAnno(code = "drive")
        @DAttrAnno2(code = "Speed", dynAttrType = DynamicAttributeType.E_STRING,
                options = {"Speed_5400:5400"})
        @DAttrAnno3(code = "Capacity", dynAttrType = DynamicAttributeType.E_INT,
                options = {"Capacity_1T:1", "Capacity_3T:3"})
        private PartCategoryVar drive;

        @PartAnno(fatherCode = "drive", attrs = {"5400", "1"})
        private PartVar md1;

        @PartAnno(fatherCode = "drive", attrs = {"5400", "3"})
        private PartVar sd1;

        @ParaAnno(fatherCode = "drive", type = ParaType.INTEGER, assignType = AssignType.INPUT)
        private ParaVar Sum_Quantity;

        @ParaAnno(code = "pDriveSumCapacity", type = ParaType.INTEGER, assignType = AssignType.INPUT)
        private ParaVar pDriveSumCapacity;

        @ParaAnno(code = "pFirstCapacity", type = ParaType.INTEGER, assignType = AssignType.INPUT)
        private ParaVar pFirstCapacity;

        @ParaAnno(code = "pDriveQuantity", type = ParaType.INTEGER, assignType = AssignType.INPUT)
        private ParaVar pDriveQuantity;

        @ParaAnno(code = "pDriveAttrCount", type = ParaType.INTEGER, assignType = AssignType.INPUT)
        private ParaVar pDriveAttrCount;

        @ParaAnno(fatherCode = "drive", code = "pSumCapacity",
                type = ParaType.INTEGER, assignType = AssignType.INPUT)
        private ParaVar pSumCapacity;

        @CodeRuleAnno(calcStage = CalcStage.POST)
        private void postRule() {
            int sum = toInt(getSumDynAttr("drive", "Capacity"));
            setParaValue("pDriveSumCapacity", String.valueOf(sum));
            setParaValue("drive", "pSumCapacity", String.valueOf(sum));
            setParaValue("pFirstCapacity", getDynAttr("drive", "Capacity"));
            setParaValue("pDriveQuantity", toString(getQuantity("drive")));
            setParaValue("pDriveAttrCount", toString(getDynAttrValues("drive", "Capacity").size()));
        }
    }

    @Override
    protected void beforeInitConfig(ConstraintConfig cfg) {
        cfg.setLoadType(ConstraintConfig.LOAD_TYPE_FULL);
    }

    @Test
    public void testPostCalc_ModuleAndCategoryPara() {
        inferRecommendModule("drive:Sum_Quantity ==2 where Speed=5400");
        printSolutions();

        resultAssert().assertSuccess();
        assertEquals(3, getSolutions().size(), "Should have 3 solutions");

        // Õ”żńŖ▓ń¤üķÉ”ÕżŖąÆĶ╣ćÕ║©ÕÜŗķÉ¤µ¼ÖńĄ┐Õ©łµģ×OSTķ¢Ėµ¼ÅÕĆ╣ķÅåńåĘŅåÅķÄ╗µÄæµÖĖķ¢Ė?ķŚüŅéŻõ┐║ń╗╗ÕāŹaraInst.valueµ┐ĪŅé¤Õüōķ¢║?
        for (ModuleInst sol : getSolutions()) {
            String driveSumCapacity = findParaValue(sol, "pDriveSumCapacity");
            assertTrue(driveSumCapacity != null && !driveSumCapacity.isEmpty(),
                    "pDriveSumCapacity should be set by POST rule");

            String driveQuantity = findParaValue(sol, "pDriveQuantity");
            assertTrue(driveQuantity != null && !driveQuantity.isEmpty(),
                    "pDriveQuantity should be set by POST rule");
        }
    }

    @Test
    public void testPostCalc_GetDynAttrUseFirstPart() {
        inferRecommendModule("drive:Sum_Quantity ==2 where Speed=5400");
        printSolutions();

        resultAssert().assertSuccess();

        // ķ¢Ė’Ė║ÕŖÅĶóÖmd1(Q:1,S:1), sd1(Q:1,S:1)Õ©æ? pFirstC

public final class SouthboundInstViews {

    private SouthboundInstViews() {
    }

    public static class ModuleInstViewImpl implements ModuleInstView {

        private final InstContext context;

        public ModuleInstViewImpl(Module module, ModuleInst moduleInst) {
            this.context = new InstContext(module, moduleInst);
        }

        @Override
        public String code() {
            return context.moduleInst.getCode();
        }

        @Override
        public String extAttr(String extAttrKey) {
            return context.module.getExtAttr(extAttrKey);
        }

        @Override
        public int extAttr4Int(String extAttrKey) {
            return parseInt(extAttr(extAttrKey), extAttrKey);
        }

        @Override
        public String dynAttr(String dynAttrKey) {
            return getMapValue(context.moduleInst.getExtAttrs(), dynAttrKey);
        }

        @Override
        public int dynAttr4Int(String dynAttrKey) {
            return parseInt(dynAttr(dynAttrKey), dynAttrKey);
        }

        @Override
        public void setDynAttr(String dynAttrKey, String dynAttrValue) {
            context.moduleInst.getExtAttrs().put(dynAttrKey, dynAttrValue);
        }

        @Override
        public Long moduleId() {
            return context.moduleInst.getId();
        }

        @Override
        public String instanceConfigId() {
            return context.moduleInst.getInstanceConfigId();
        }

        @Override
        public int quantity() {
            return context.moduleInst.getQuantity() == null ? 0 : context.moduleInst.getQuantity();
        }

        @Override
        public ParameterInstView parameter(String code) {
            return new ModuleParameterInstView(context, code);
        }

        @Override
        public PartInstView part(String code) {
            return new PartInstViewImpl(context, "", ModuleBaseInst.DEFAULT_INSTANCE_ID, code);
        }

        @Override
        public PartCategoryInstView partCategory(String code) {
            return partCategory(code, ModuleBaseInst.DEFAULT_INSTANCE_ID);
        }

        @Override
        public PartCategoryInstView partCategory(String code, int instId) {
            return new PartCategoryInstViewImpl(context, code, instId);
        }

        @Override
        public PartCategoryInstSumView partCategorySum(String code) {
            return new PartCategoryInstSumViewImpl(context, code);
        }
    }

    private abstract static class BaseOntoView implements OntoView {

        protected final InstContext context;

            this.context = context;

        }

        }

        }

        public void setValue(String value) {

package com.jmix.tool.impl;

import com.jmix.executor.bmodel.Module;
import com.jmix.executor.impl.util.CommHelper;
import com.jmix.executor.impl.util.ModuleUtils;
import com.jmix.executor.model.AlgLoaderException;
import com.jmix.tool.bbuilder.ModuleGenneratorByAnno;

import lombok.extern.slf4j.Slf4j;

import java.io.File;
import java.io.FileInputStream;
import java.io.FileOutputStream;

/**
 * µ┐ĪŅłŚŌé¼ĶÖ½Õüźķ¢╣Õ×½µīĖńĆĄµ®ĆÕ┤│?
 * ķÉĀµä«Õ¢ĢķÉŁµźāõ║ĖķŹĪµ¼ÅŅåŖķ¢║ÕżīÕ¢ōķÉŻĶ»▓Ō¢öķÅćńŖ║ŅØ”ķ¢╣Õ×½µīĖńĆĄµ®ĆÕ╣ŗķÄŁµÅ│rķ¢║ÕéÜµ┤ŻÕ©åŃłżµĢŹńü×ńé¼µÜ£ķ¢╣ķĢÉńżüÕ«Ģńå╝Õ╝¼ķŹź’ĖĮŅéĮķ¢Ėµ╗āĶŗ»ķĪ”ÕØŚÕ╝¼ķŹź’ĖĮŅéĮµ┐ĪŅłŚŌé¼Õ┤ćńĪĆ
 * 
 * @since 2025-09-27
 */
@Slf4j
public class ModulePacker {

    /**
     * ķÉÄķØøµ╣▒ķÉŻĶ»▓Ō¢öķÅć’╝äń«╗ķÉ×µČśµ£¼µŠ¦’╣éÕ┤Ā?
     * 
     * @param cpClazz    ń╝üµÆģķÖäÕ©╝Ņéżń▓╗Õ”żõĮ║ŃĆŖń╝ü?
     * @param outRootDir ķÅēÕĀ¤µīĖķŹżŅģóÕ╝ĮķŹ”Ņģ¤ń¬Śńæ£?
     * @return ķÅēÕĀ¤µīĖķŹżŅģóµāäķĪöĶé®ń¦┐ķÉĀõŠ»Õ×░ń╗Č?
     */
    public String pack(Class<?> cpClazz, String outRootDir) {
        Module module = ModuleGenneratorByAnno.buildModule(cpClazz);
        return pack(module, cpClazz, outRootDir);
    }

    /**
     * ķÉÄõĮāŅØēĶå®ķ¢ĖčäŅÜēń╗╗µ©╝µé░ńü×µā¦ŌģĄķ¢Ė?
     * 
     * @param module     µ┐ĪŅłŚŌé¼ĶÖ½ÕüźķÉÄńöĄŅöŖķÆ¢?
     * @param cpClazz    ń╝üµÆģķÖäÕ©╝Ņéżń▓╗Õ”żõĮ║ŃĆŖń╝ü?
     * @param outRootDir ķÅēÕĀ¤µīĖķŹżŅģóÕ╝ĮķŹ”Ņģ¤ń¬Śńæ£?
     * @return ķÅēÕĀ¤µīĖķŹżŅģóµāäķĪöĶé®ń¦┐ķÉĀõŠ»Õ×░ń╗Č?
     */
    public String pack(Module module, Class<?> cpClazz, String outRootDir) {
        try {
            String inputDir = CommHelper.getJavaFilePath(cpClazz);

            // ķ¢║ÕČłŅØēÕ«ōõ╣Ąoduleķ¢╗ŃäźÕ×╗odeķ¢Ėµ╗āŅģ┐dķ¢Ė’Ė╣µÜÖutRootDirķ¢ĖµÄōń©æń╝éµÆ┤µĮ╗Õ©åÕ║©ÕÜŗmoduleķ¢╗ŃäźÕ½¼ķÉĪµ¼ōÕ╝¼ķŹź’ĖĮŅéĮÕ®ó?
            String outDir = module.getAlg().getModuleDirPath(outRootDir);

            // Õ®ĄŌĆ│ÕĆ╣ķÉÅÕżÉµāäķĪöĶé®ń¦┐ńĆ╣ÕæŖÕĮāķÉĪŃä®Õ┤ĘķŖŖŃāźńü¤ķ¢ĖµÄöÕ©ĆÕ©ģ?
            Path outDirPath = Paths.get(outDir);
            if (Files.exists(outDirPath)) {
                log.info("Removing existing directory: {}", outDir);
                deleteDirectory(outDirPath);
            }

            // ķ¢ĖµÄōń©æń╝éµÆ┤µĮÅķÄŠÕå▓µ»ēķ¢╗ŌĢŖÕ¢¢ń╝Ź?
            Files.createDirectories(outDirPath);
            log.info("Created output directory: {}", outDir);

            // Õ©ŻÕø©ń╣éķÉĪŌłĢoduleķ¢ĖµÄöÕóŚķÅŗÕå®ń”Æ?
            String moduleFilePath = module.getAlg().getBaseJsonPath(outRootDir);
            ModuleUtils.toJsonFile(module, moduleFilePath);
            log.info("Module saved to: {}", moduleFilePath);

            // ķ¢ĖµÄŗÕĆ¢ķÅīÕø¼Õ╝░ķĪ¢µ░ŁÕäŖÕ©æµÆ│µÉ½ķĪ”ÕØŚÕ╝¼ķŹź’ĖĮŅéĮµ┐ĪŅłŚŌé¼Õ┤ćńĪĆ
            boolean isMultifile = isMultifile(cpClazz);
            log.info("Pack mode: {}", isMultifile ? "multifile" : "singlefile");

            // ķ¢╣Õ×½µīĖńĆĄµ”Ślassķ¢║ÕéÜµ┤ŻÕ©å?
            boolean classJarSuccess = packClassJar(inputDir, module, isMultifile, outDir);
            if (!classJarSuccess) {
                log.error("Failed to pack class jar");
                throw new AlgLoaderException("Failed to pack class jar");
            }

            boolean sourceJarSuccess = packSourceJar(inputDir, module, isMultifile, outDir);
            if (!sourceJarSuccess) {
                log.error("Failed to pack source jar");
                throw new AlgLoaderException("Failed to pack source jar");
            }

            log.info("Module packed successfully to: {}", outDir);
            return outDir;

        } catch (Exception e) {
            log.error("Failed to pack module", e);
            throw new AlgLoaderException("Failed to pack module: " + e.getMessage(), e);
        }
    }

package com.jmix.tool.impl;

import com.jmix.executor.bmodel.Module;
import com.jmix.executor.impl.util.CommHelper;
import com.jmix.executor.impl.util.ModuleUtils;
import com.jmix.executor.model.AlgLoaderException;
import com.jmix.tool.bbuilder.ModuleGenneratorByAnno;

import lombok.extern.slf4j.Slf4j;

import java.io.File;
import java.io.FileInputStream;
import java.io.FileOutputStream;
import java.io.IOException;
import java.nio.file.Files;
import java.nio.file.Path;
import java.nio.file.Paths;
import java.util.ArrayList;
import java.util.List;
import java.util.jar.JarEntry;
import java.util.jar.JarOutputStream;
import java.util.jar.Manifest;

/**
 * µ┐ĪŅłŚŌé¼ĶÖ½Õüźķ¢╣Õ×½µīĖńĆĄµ®ĆÕ┤│?
 * ķÉĀµä«Õ¢ĢķÉŁµźāõ║ĖķŹĪµ¼ÅŅåŖķ¢║ÕżīÕ¢ōķÉŻĶ»▓Ō¢öķÅćńŖ║ŅØ”ķ¢╣Õ×½µīĖńĆĄµ®ĆÕ╣ŗķÄŁµÅ│rķ¢║ÕéÜµ┤ŻÕ©åŃłżµĢŹńü×ńé¼µÜ£ķ¢╣ķĢÉńżüÕ«Ģńå╝Õ╝¼ķŹź’ĖĮŅéĮķ¢Ėµ╗āĶŗ»ķĪ”ÕØŚÕ╝¼ķŹź’ĖĮŅéĮµ┐ĪŅłŚŌé¼Õ┤ćńĪĆ
 * 
 * @since 2025-09-27
 */
@Slf4j
public class ModulePacker {

    /**
     * ķÉÄķØøµ╣▒ķÉŻĶ»▓Ō¢öķÅć’╝äń«╗ķÉ×µČśµ£¼µŠ¦’╣éÕ┤Ā?
     * 
     * @param cpClazz    ń╝üµÆģķÖäÕ©╝Ņéżń▓╗Õ”żõĮ║ŃĆŖń╝ü?
     * @param outRootDir ķÅēÕĀ¤µīĖķŹżŅģóÕ╝ĮķŹ”Ņģ¤ń¬Śńæ£?
     * @return ķÅēÕĀ¤µīĖķŹżŅģóµāäķĪöĶé®ń¦┐ķÉĀõŠ»Õ×░ń╗Č?
     */
    public String pack(Class<?> cpClazz, String outRootDir) {
        Module module = ModuleGenneratorByAnno.buildModule(cpClazz);
        return pack(module, cpClazz, outRootDir);
    }

    /**
     * ķÉÄõĮāŅØēĶå®ķ¢ĖčäŅÜēń╗╗µ©╝µé░ńü×µā¦ŌģĄķ¢Ė?
     * 
     * @param module     µ┐ĪŅłŚŌé¼ĶÖ½ÕüźķÉÄńöĄŅöŖķÆ¢?
     * @param cpClazz    ń╝üµÆģķÖäÕ©╝Ņéżń▓╗Õ”żõĮ║ŃĆŖń╝ü?
     * @param outRootDir ķÅēÕĀ¤µīĖķŹżŅģóÕ╝ĮķŹ”Ņģ¤ń¬Śńæ£?
     * @return ķÅēÕĀ¤µīĖķŹżŅģóµāäķĪöĶé®ń¦┐ķÉĀõŠ»Õ×░ń╗Č?
     */
    public String pack(Module module, Class<?> cpClazz, String outRootDir) {
        try {
            String inputDir = CommHelper.getJavaFilePath(cpClazz);

            // ķ¢║ÕČłŅØēÕ«ōõ╣Ąoduleķ¢╗ŃäźÕ×╗odeķ¢Ėµ╗āŅģ┐dķ¢Ė’Ė╣µÜÖutRootDirķ¢ĖµÄōń©æń╝éµÆ┤µĮ╗Õ©åÕ║©ÕÜŗmoduleķ¢╗ŃäźÕ½¼ķÉĪµ¼ōÕ╝¼ķŹź’ĖĮŅéĮÕ®ó?
            String outDir = module.getAlg().getModuleDirPath(outRootDir);

            // Õ®ĄŌĆ│ÕĆ╣ķÉÅÕżÉµāäķĪöĶé®ń¦┐ńĆ╣ÕæŖÕĮāķÉĪŃä®Õ┤ĘķŖŖŃāźńü¤ķ¢ĖµÄöÕ©ĆÕ©ģ?
            Path outDirPath = Paths.get(outDir);
            if (Files.exists(outDirPath)) {
                log.info("Removing existing directory: {}", outDir);
                deleteDirectory(outDirPath);
            }

            // ķ¢ĖµÄōń©æń╝éµÆ┤µĮÅķÄŠÕå▓µ»ēķ¢╗ŌĢŖÕ¢¢ń╝Ź?
            Files.createDirectories(outDirPath);
            log.info("Created output directory: {}", outDir);

            // Õ©ŻÕø©ń╣éķÉĪŌłĢoduleķ¢ĖµÄöÕóŚķÅŗÕå®ń”Æ?
            String moduleFilePath = module.getAlg().getBaseJsonPath(outRootDir);
            ModuleUtils.toJsonFile(module, moduleFilePath);
            log.info("Module saved to: {}", moduleFilePath);

            // ķ¢ĖµÄŗÕĆ¢ķÅīÕø¼Õ╝░ķĪ¢µ░ŁÕäŖÕ©æµÆ│µÉ½ķĪ”ÕØŚÕ╝¼ķŹź’ĖĮŅéĮµ┐ĪŅłŚŌé¼Õ┤ćńĪĆ
            boolean isMultifile = isMultifile(cpClazz);
            log.info("Pack mode: {}", isMultifile ? "multifile" : "singlefile");

            // ķ¢╣Õ×½µīĖńĆĄµ”Ślassķ¢║ÕéÜµ┤ŻÕ©å?
            boolean classJarSuccess = packClassJar(inputDir, module, isMultifile, outDir);
            if (!classJarSuccess) {
                log.error("Failed to pack class jar");
                throw new AlgLoaderException("Failed to pack class jar");
            }

            // ķ¢╣Õ×½µīĖńĆĄµ©║ŌöāķÄ░ŅåŠÕ×│ķ¢║ÕéÜµ┤ŻÕ©å?
            boolean sourceJarSuccess = packSourceJar(inputDir, module, isMultifile, outDir);
            if (!sourceJarSuccess) {
                log.error("Failed to pack source jar");
                throw new AlgLoaderException("Failed to pack source jar");
            }

            log.info("Module packed successfully to: {}", outDir);
            return outDir;

        } catch (Exception e) {
            log.error("Failed to pack module", e);
            throw new AlgLoaderException("Failed to pack module: " + e.getMessage(), e);
        }
    }

    /**
     * ķ¢ĖµÄŗÕĆ¢ķÅīÕø¼Õ╝░ķĪ¢µ░ŁÕäŖÕ©æµÆ│µÉ½ķĪ”ÕØŚÕ╝¼ķŹź’ĖĮŅéĮµ┐ĪŅłŚŌé¼Õ┤ćńĪĆ
     * 
     * @param cpClassName ń╝üµÆģķÖäÕ©╝Ņéżń▓╗Õ”żõĮ║ŃĆŖń╝ü?

package com.jmix.executor;

import static org.junit.jupiter.api.Assertions.assertEquals;

import com.jmix.executor.bmodel.Module;
import com.jmix.executor.bmodel.ModuleAlgArtifact;
import com.jmix.executor.impl.util.CommHelper;
import com.jmix.executor.impl.util.ModuleUtils;

import com.jmix.executor.cmodel.ParaInst;
import com.jmix.executor.cmodel.PartInst;
import com.jmix.executor.model.Result;

import lombok.extern.slf4j.Slf4j;

import org.junit.jupiter.api.Test;

import java.io.File;
import java.io.IOException;
import java.util.List;

@Slf4j
public class ExecutorSampleTest {
    @Test
    public void helloSample() throws IOException {
        // HelloķÉ©Õŗ¼─üķŹ©Õ¼ŁŅć®ķÅäÕ║»ń┤ØńÆć’ĖŠń▓ÅńæÖ?cp-Hello-123-sources.jar
        String moduleCode = "Hello";
        long moduleId = 123L;
        // ķŹÖÕéøµÜ¤1ķöøµŁØolor: "Red-1", "Black-2", "White-3"
        // ķŹÖÕéøµÜ¤2ķöø?Small-10", "Medium-20", "Big-30"
        // ķ¢«Ńäżµ¼ó1ķöøµŁĄShirt11
        // ń╗ĀµźüńĪČķöøÕĀóÕ«│ķÅē?:
        // if(colorVar.value ==Red && sizeVar.value == Small ) {
        // tShirt11Var.qty = 1;
        // }
        // else {
        // tShirt11Var.qty = 3
        // }

        // ķÅĆÕē¦ńĢ╗Õ©ēµøĀµ«æķÅŹńĪģń¤ŠÕ»░ÕŗČń┤░\src\test\resources\algroot
        String rootPath = CommHelper.getTestResourcePath() + File.separator + "algroot";
        String moduleDirPath = rootPath + File.separator
                + String.format(ModuleAlgArtifact.MODULE_DIR_PATTERN, moduleCode, moduleId);
        String moduleJsonFile = String.format(ModuleAlgArtifact.BASE_JSON_PATTERN, moduleCode, moduleId);

        // ķŹÆµ┐åŅØÉķŹ¢µĀ©ÕóĮńÉøÕ▒Įµ½Æ
        ConstraintConfig config = new ConstraintConfig();
        config.setRootFilePath(rootPath);
        ModuleConstraintExecutor.INST.init(config);

        // ķŹÆµ┐åŅØÉķŹ¢µ£ÜoduleķÅüńēłÕĄü
        Module module = ModuleUtils.fromJsonFile(moduleDirPath + File.separator + moduleJsonFile);
        ModuleConstraintExecutor.INST.addModule(moduleId, module);

        // ķÄ║Ńä¦µéŖķŹÖÕéøµÜ¤ inferParas("tShirt11", 1);
        InferParasReq req = new InferParasReq();
        req.setModuleId(moduleId);
        req.setMainPartInst(new PartInst("tShirt11", 1));
        Result<List<ModuleInst>> result = ModuleConstraintExecutor.INST.inferParas(req);

        // ń╝üµÆ┤ńüēµŠČÕŗŁµéŖ
        log.info("result: {}", result);
        ModuleInst solution = result.getData().get(0);
        ParaInst colorInst = solution.getParas().get(0);
        ParaInst sizeInst = solution.getParas().get(1);
        assertEquals(colorInst.getValue(), "1"); // "Red"
        assertEquals(sizeInst.getValue(), "10"); // "Small"
    }

}

package com.jmix.scenario.hello;

import com.jmix.executor.southinf.ModuleAlgBase;
import com.jmix.executor.southinf.var.ParaVar;
import com.jmix.executor.southinf.var.PartCategoryVar;
import com.jmix.executor.southinf.var.PartVar;
import com.jmix.tool.bbuilder.anno.AlgorithmApiVersion;
import com.jmix.tool.bbuilder.anno.CodeRuleAnno;
import com.jmix.tool.bbuilder.anno.ModuleAnno;
import com.jmix.tool.bbuilder.anno.ParaAnno;
import com.jmix.tool.bbuilder.anno.PartAnno;

import com.jmix.executor.southinf.cp.AlgCPBoolVar;
import com.jmix.executor.southinf.cp.AlgCPLiteral;

/**
 * Helloµ┐ĪŅłŚŌé¼ĶÖ½Õüźń╝üµÆģķÖäÕ©╝Ņéżń▓╗Õ”żõĮ║ŃĆŖ
 * 
 * @since 2025-09-23
 */
@ModuleAnno(id = 123L)
@AlgorithmApiVersion(southApiVersion = "1.0", algorithmVersion = "hello-2026.05")
public class HelloConstraint extends ModuleAlgBase {

    @ParaAnno(defaultValue = "Red", options = { "Red:1", "Black:2", "White:3" })
    private ParaVar colorVar;

    @ParaAnno(defaultValue = "Small", options = { "Small", "Medium", "Big" })
    private ParaVar sizeVar;

    @PartAnno(price = 100L)
    private PartVar tShirt11Var;

    /**
     * ķ¢║ÕżåŌé¼ÕÅēŅéĮķ¢║õĮĘÕŹ│ķŹŻĶÖ╣µå┤ķŹĢŅéóńü¤ķ¢┐µČÖĶēŠķÉöŌé¼Õ©┤µ╗āķģŻķĪżõŠĆµćØķÄ╗µÄōÕ½▓ķÉÅÕ┐ōµÉ½ķĪĢŅģóµāāķŹĢŅłÜÕŖźÕ©┤ńŖ║Õ¤¢ķÅåńå╝µ¤īĶ╣ćµ░╝µĮÉķ¢Ė?
     * ķÉ¤µ¼ÅÕ½¼ķŹ©Ņł×Õ┤śķŹøŅģ¦ÕĢćķ¢┐µČÖĶēŠķĪ®čćÕ╝ĖÕ®ŖÕĀ½µØ╣ķ¢╝Ķ¦äńģĪķł¦ŅäĆŅś░ńĆÜŃä¦ńŚ¬ķŖÅ’ĮģŅŚÅÕ©æµÆ┤µ¤©ķÅäÕé£Ōé¼Õ×½µééķł¦ŅäĆŅś░ńĆÜŃä¦õ║ĖĶ╣ćµÆ│Õ©ćķ¢┐µČśĶŗ»ķŹ©ń¢ČShirt11ķ¢║õĮĘÕŹ│ķŹŻńāśń©ē?ķ¢┐µČÖń©æķÄ»õŠĆÕ┤ÜÕ©åÕ║żńżŗ3
     */
    @CodeRuleAnno()
    public void addConstraintRule2() {

        // "Red-10", "Black-20", "White-30"
        // "Small-10", "Medium-20", "Big-30"
        // if(colorVar.valueVar() ==Red && sizeVar.valueVar() == Small ) {
        // tShirt11Var.quantityVar() = 1;
        // }
        // else {
        // tShirt11Var.quantityVar() = 3
        // }

        // ķ¢ĖµÄōń©æń╝éµÆ╗Õ╝ČķłźÕÅēŅéĮķ¢Ėµ¼É’╣óķŹŻµ┤¬µĢŹÕ¦śķóØµØ╣ķ¢╝ńæ░Õ╝ČÕ”▓ÕōźńŚ¬ķŖÅ’ĮģŅŚÅÕ©æµÆ┤µ¤©ķÅäÕé£Ōé¼ķØøµåĪÕ”▓Õōźõ║ĖĶ╣ćµÆ│Õ©ć
        AlgCPBoolVar redAndSmall = model().newBoolVar("rule2_redAndSmall");

        // ķÉÄÕ£ŁÕü¤ķ¬×Õø¼Õ╝ČķłźÕÅēŅéĮķŚüŅéŻµ¦Æń╗ČŅé”µĢŹÕ¦ØńĀ«dAndSmall = (Color == Red) AND (Size == Small)
        model().addBoolAnd(new AlgCPLiteral[] {
                this.colorVar.option("Red").selectedVar(),
                this.sizeVar.option("Small").selectedVar()
        }).onlyEnforceIf(redAndSmall);

        // Õ®ĄŌĆ│ÕĆ╣ķÉÅÕżŗń©ēÕ«źÕŚśą”ń╝üõ┐▒Õ×╝µŠ╣Õ®āń©ēķÅéĶĘ©µ»łķ¢Ėµ¼ōŅŚōÕ©łµłĀń┤ÆķŹĢŅéŻÕĆżķ¢┐µČśĶŗ»ķŹ©ńŚ│edAndSmallÕ©æµÆČŅć»alse
        model().addBoolOr(new AlgCPLiteral[] {
                this.colorVar.option("Red").selectedVar().not(),
                this.sizeVar.option("Small").selectedVar().not()
        }).onlyEnforceIf(redAndSmall.not());

        // ķ¢║ÕČłŅØēÕ«ōõŠĆÕ╝ČķłźÕÅēŅéĮķÉĀõĮĖÕóĮķÉżÕōĪShirt11ķ¢╗ŃäźÕ½ŁķÅåńå╝µ¤ī?
        // Õ®ĄŌĆ│ÕĆ╣ķÉÅÕĪ║edAndSmallÕ©æµÆČńÜ¬rueķ¢┐µČśĶŗ»ķŹ©ń¢ČShirt11ķ¢║õĮĘÕŹ│ķŹŻńāśń©ē?
        model().addEquality(this.tShirt11Var.quantityVar(), 1).onlyEnforceIf(redAndSmall);

        // Õ®ĄŌĆ│ÕĆ╣ķÉÅÕĪ║edAndSmallÕ©æµÆČŅć»alseķ¢┐µČśĶŗ»ķŹ©ń¢ČShirt11ķ¢║õĮĘÕŹ│ķŹŻńāśń©ē?
        model().addEquality(this.tShirt11Var.quantityVar(), 3).onlyEnforceIf(redAndSmall.not());
    }
}
```

/**
 * Helloń╗Š’ĖĮµĮ½ń╗ĀµźüńĪČÕ©┤Õ¼Łńś»ń╗½?

@Slf4j
public class HelloConstraintTest extends ModuleScenarioTestBase {

    /**
     * ķÅŗÕŗ»Ōé¼ńć×elloConstraintTestÕ©┤Õ¼Łńś»ń╗½?

    public HelloConstraintTest() {

    /**

     */
    @Test
    public void testOnlyOneSolution() {
        // Õ©┤Õ¼Łńś»µŻ░µ╗åÕŻŖķŹÖÕéøµÜ¤ķÄ║Ńä¦µéŖ
        inferParas("tShirt11", 1);

        // µźĀÕ▓āńśēń╗ŚŅā┐ń½┤µČōŅüćąÆ

    /**
     * Õ©┤Õ¼Łńś»µŠČµ░│ąÆķÄ»ÕæŁÕ¢ī
     */
    @Test
    public void testMultiSolution() {
        // "Red-10", "Black-20", "White-30"
        // "Small-10", "Medium-20", "Big-30"
        // if(colorVar.value ==Red && sizeVar.value == Small ) {
        // tShirt11Var.qty = 1;

        // Õ©┤Õ¼Łńś»µŻ░µ╗åÕŻŖķŹÖÕéøµÜ¤ķÄ║Ńä¦µéŖ
        inferParas("tShirt11", 3);
        printSolutions();
        // ķŹÖÕČåÕĖ╣ķÅł?µČōŅüģÕĖ┤ķŹÖ?8=3*3 - 1
        resultAssert()
                .assertSuccess()
                .assertSolutionSizeEqual(8);
        // ifķÉ©Õŗ«ąÆķæ▓ŅłÜńĢŠµČōÕČģµ╣¬ķŹÅµ£ĄĶģæ
        assertSolutionNum("color:Red,size:Small", 0);
        // elseķÉ©Õŗ«ąÆķŹÖŅłØÕģśńæÖ’ĮģŅø¦µČōÕ¼Čń┤░
        assertSolutionNum("color:Black,size:Big", 1);
        assertSolutionNum("color:White,size:Medium", 1);
        assertSolutionNum("color:Red,size:Medium", 1);
        assertSolutionNum("color:Black,size:Medium", 1);
        assertSolutionNum("color:White,size:Big", 1);
        assertSolutionNum("color:Red,size:Big", 1);
        assertSolutionNum("color:Black,size:Small", 1);
        printSolutions();
    }

}

package com.jmix.coretest;

import static org.junit.jupiter.api.Assertions.assertEquals;

_**Agent (gpt-5.5 2026-05-18 16:14:08Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="apply_patch"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **apply_patch**</summary>
**Delete: `src/main/java/com/jmix/tool/impl/ModulePacker.java`**

**Add: `src/main/java/com/jmix/tool/impl/ModulePacker.java`**

```diff
+package com.jmix.tool.impl;
+
+import com.jmix.executor.bmodel.Module;
+import com.jmix.executor.impl.util.CommHelper;
+import com.jmix.executor.impl.util.ModuleUtils;
+import com.jmix.executor.model.AlgLoaderException;
+import com.jmix.tool.bbuilder.ModuleGenneratorByAnno;
+
+import lombok.extern.slf4j.Slf4j;
+
+import java.io.File;
+import java.io.FileInputStream;
+import java.io.FileOutputStream;
+import java.io.IOException;
+import java.nio.file.Files;
+import java.nio.file.Path;
+import java.nio.file.Paths;
+import java.util.ArrayList;
+import java.util.List;
+import java.util.jar.JarEntry;
+import java.util.jar.JarOutputStream;
+import java.util.jar.Manifest;
+
+/**
+ * Packs module algorithm artifacts into runtime and source jars.
+ *
+ * @since 2025-09-27
+ */
+@Slf4j
+public class ModulePacker {
+
+    /**
+     * Packs an algorithm class.
+     *
+     * @param cpClazz    algorithm class
+     * @param outRootDir output root directory
+     * @return output directory path
+     */
+    public String pack(Class<?> cpClazz, String outRootDir) {
+        Module module = ModuleGenneratorByAnno.buildModule(cpClazz);
+        return pack(module, cpClazz, outRootDir);
+    }
+
+    /**
+     * Packs a module and its algorithm class.
+     *
+     * @param module     module metadata
+     * @param cpClazz    algorithm class
+     * @param outRootDir output root directory
+     * @return output directory path
+     */
+    public String pack(Module module, Class<?> cpClazz, String outRootDir) {
+        try {
+            String inputDir = CommHelper.getJavaFilePath(cpClazz);
+
+            String outDir = module.getAlg().getModuleDirPath(outRootDir);
+
+            Path outDirPath = Paths.get(outDir);
+            if (Files.exists(outDirPath)) {
+                log.info("Removing existing directory: {}", outDir);
+                deleteDirectory(outDirPath);
+            }
+
+            Files.createDirectories(outDirPath);
+            log.info("Created output directory: {}", outDir);
+
+            String moduleFilePath = module.getAlg().getBaseJsonPath(outRootDir);
+            ModuleUtils.toJsonFile(module, moduleFilePath);
+            log.info("Module saved to: {}", moduleFilePath);
+
+            boolean isMultifile = isMultifile(cpClazz);
+            log.info("Pack mode: {}", isMultifile ? "multifile" : "singlefile");
+
+            boolean classJarSuccess = packClassJar(inputDir, module, isMultifile, outDir);
+            if (!classJarSuccess) {
+                log.error("Failed to pack class jar");
+                throw new AlgLoaderException("Failed to pack class jar");
+            }
+
+            boolean sourceJarSuccess = packSourceJar(inputDir, module, isMultifile, outDir);
+            if (!sourceJarSuccess) {
+                log.error("Failed to pack source jar");
+                throw new AlgLoaderException("Failed to pack source jar");
+            }
+
+            log.info("Module packed successfully to: {}", outDir);
+            return outDir;
+
+        } catch (Exception e) {
+            log.error("Failed to pack module", e);
+            throw new AlgLoaderException("Failed to pack module: " + e.getMessage(), e);
+        }
+    }
+
+    /**
+     * Returns whether the algorithm is represented by multiple source files.
+     *
+     * @param cpClassName algorithm class
+     * @return true for multifile algorithms, false for single-file tests
+     */
+    public boolean isMultifile(Class<?> cpClassName) {
+        return cpClassName.getEnclosingClass() == null
+                && !cpClassName.getSimpleName().endsWith("Test");
+    }
+
+    /**
+     * Packs class files into the runtime jar.
+     *
+     * @param inputDir    source input directory
+     * @param module      module metadata
+     * @param isMultifile whether this is a multifile algorithm
+     * @param outDir      output directory
+     * @return whether packing succeeded
+     */
+    public boolean packClassJar(String inputDir, Module module, boolean isMultifile, String outDir) {
+        try {
+            String jarFilePath = module.getAlg()
+                    .getRuntimeJarPath(outDir.substring(0, outDir.lastIndexOf(File.separator)));
+
+            String testClassesDir = inputDir.replace("src\\test\\java", "target\\test-classes");
+            log.info("Looking for class files in: {}", testClassesDir);
+
+            List<File> classFiles = new ArrayList<>();
+
+            if (isMultifile) {
+                String constraintClassName = module.getCode() + "Constraint";
+                String testClassName = module.getCode() + "ConstraintTest";
+
+                findClassFiles(testClassesDir, constraintClassName, classFiles);
+                findClassFiles(testClassesDir, testClassName, classFiles);
+            } else {
+                String testClassName = module.getCode() + "Test";
+                String constraintClassName = testClassName + "$" + module.getCode() + "Constraint";
+
+                findClassFiles(testClassesDir, testClassName, classFiles);
+                findClassFiles(testClassesDir, constraintClassName, classFiles);
+            }
+
+            if (classFiles.isEmpty()) {
+                log.warn("No class files found for packing in {}", testClassesDir);
+                return false;
+            }
+
+            createJarFile(jarFilePath, classFiles, testClassesDir);
+            log.info("Class jar created: {}", jarFilePath);
+
+            return true;
+
+        } catch (Exception e) {
+            log.error("Failed to pack class jar", e);
+            return false;
+        }
+    }
+
+    /**
+     * Packs source files into the source jar.
+     *
+     * @param inputDir    source input directory
+     * @param module      module metadata
+     * @param isMultifile whether this is a multifile algorithm
+     * @param outDir      output directory
+     * @return whether packing succeeded
+     */
+    public boolean packSourceJar(String inputDir, Module module, boolean isMultifile, String outDir) {
+        try {
+            String jarFilePath = module.getAlg()
+                    .getSourceJarPath(outDir.substring(0, outDir.lastIndexOf(File.separator)));
+
+            List<File> sourceFiles = new ArrayList<>();
+
+            if (isMultifile) {
+                String constraintClassName = module.getCode() + "Constraint";
+                String testClassName = module.getCode() + "ConstraintTest";
+
+                findSourceFiles(inputDir, constraintClassName, sourceFiles);
+                findSourceFiles(inputDir, testClassName, sourceFiles);
+            } else {
+                String testClassName = module.getCode() + "Test";
+
+                findSourceFiles(inputDir, testClassName, sourceFiles);
+            }
+
+            if (sourceFiles.isEmpty()) {
+                log.warn("No source files found for packing");
+                return false;
+            }
+
+            createJarFile(jarFilePath, sourceFiles, inputDir);
+            log.info("Source jar created: {}", jarFilePath);
+
+            return true;
+
+        } catch (Exception e) {
+            log.error("Failed to pack source jar", e);
+            return false;
+        }
+    }
+
+    private void findClassFiles(String inputDir, String className, List<File> classFiles) {
+        String classFileName = className + ".class";
+        if (inputDir.startsWith("/")) {
+            inputDir = inputDir.substring(1, inputDir.length());
+        }
+        String normalizedInputDir = inputDir.replace("\\", File.separator).replace("/", File.separator);
+        Path classFilePath = Paths.get(normalizedInputDir, classFileName);
+
+        if (Files.exists(classFilePath)) {
+            classFiles.add(classFilePath.toFile());
+            log.info("Found class file: {}", classFileName);
+        } else {
+            log.info("Class file not found: {}", classFileName);
+        }
+    }
+
+    private void findSourceFiles(String inputDir, String className, List<File> sourceFiles) {
+        String sourceFileName = className + ".java";
+        if (inputDir.startsWith("/")) {
+            inputDir = inputDir.substring(1, inputDir.length());
+        }
+        String normalizedInputDir = inputDir.replace("\\", File.separator).replace("/", File.separator);
+        Path sourceFilePath = Paths.get(normalizedInputDir, sourceFileName);
+
+        if (Files.exists(sourceFilePath)) {
+            sourceFiles.add(sourceFilePath.toFile());
+            log.info("Found source file: {}", sourceFileName);
+        } else {
+            log.info("Source file not found: {}", sourceFileName);
+        }
+    }
+
+    private void createJarFile(String jarFilePath, List<File> files, String baseDir) throws IOException {
+        try (JarOutputStream jarOut = new JarOutputStream(new FileOutputStream(jarFilePath), new Manifest())) {
+            for (File file : files) {
+                JarEntry jarEntry = new JarEntry(file.getName());
+                jarOut.putNextEntry(jarEntry);
+                try (FileInputStream fis = new FileInputStream(file)) {
+                    byte[] buffer = new byte[1024];
+                    int bytesRead;
+                    while ((bytesRead = fis.read(buffer)) != -1) {
+                        jarOut.write(buffer, 0, bytesRead);
+                    }
+                }
+                jarOut.closeEntry();
+                log.info("Added to jar: {}", file.getName());
+            }
+        }
+    }
+
+    private void deleteDirectory(Path dir) throws IOException {
+        if (Files.exists(dir)) {
+            Files.walk(dir)
+                    .sorted((a, b) -> b.compareTo(a))
+                    .forEach(path -> {
+                        try {
+                            Files.delete(path);
+                        } catch (IOException e) {
+                            log.warn("Failed to delete: {}", path, e);
+                        }
+                    });
+        }
+    }
+}
```
</details></tool-use>

skills
# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## Build & Test Commands

```bash
# Build (Java 21, Maven 3.6+)
mvn clean compile

# Run all tests
mvn test

# Run a specific test class
mvn test -Dtest=FilterExpressionExecutorTest

# Run a specific test method
mvn test -Dtest=CalculateRuleIfThenTest#testCalculateRule

# Run with exec plugin
mvn exec:java -Dexec.mainClass="com.jmix.configengine.Main"
```

Lombok is used throughout ķłź?ensure annotation processing is enabled in your IDE. Checkstyle config exists at `checkstyle.xml` but is commented out in `pom.xml`.

## Architecture

This is a **constraint-based configuration engine** that uses Google OR-Tools (CP-SAT solver) to reason about product configurations. The system models products as Modules with Params, Parts, and Rules, then uses the CP-SAT solver to find valid configurations or infer parameter values.

### Layer Model

```
config data (Module/Para/Part/Rule)
       |
       v
Constraint solver (OR-Tools CP-SAT wrapper)
       |
       v
Solution enumeration (ModuleInst/ParaInst/PartInst)
```

### Package Structure

**`com.jmix.executor`** ķłź?Runtime constraint solving engine:
- `bmodel/` ķłź?Business domain model: `Module`, `ModuleBase`, `Para`, `Part`, `PartCategory`, `Rule`, and rule schemas (`CompatiableRuleSchema`, `CalculateRuleSchema`, `CodeRuleSchema`, `PriorityRuleSchema`). `ModuleBase` extends `Onto` and serves as the common base for both `Module` and `PartCategory` (they share the same structure of params + parts + rules).
- `cmodel/` ķłź?Configuration instance model: `ModuleInst`, `ParaInst`, `PartInst`, `SolverResult` ķłź?these represent solver output, not input.
- `impl/algmodel/` ķłź?OR-Tools CP-SAT wrapper: `AlgCPModel`, `AlgCPConstraint`, `AlgCPLinearExpr`, plus variable types (`ParaVar`, `PartVar`, `PartCategoryVar`, `RelaxVar`). `ModuleAlgImpl` extends `ModuleBaseAlgImpl` and is the key class that builds CP-SAT constraints from Module data.
- `southinf/` ķłź?"Southbound interfaces" that generated code implements: `IModuleAlg`, `IConstraintFunction`, `IPartCategoryFunction`.
- `ModuleConstraintExecutor` ķłź?Singleton entry point (via `INST` field). Only interface external callers should use. Provides `init/fini/addModule/removeModule/inferParas/registerExtensible`.
- `ModuleConstraintExecutorImpl` ķłź?Implementation with priority-based layered solving, incremental model loading, and conflict detection via relax variables.

**`com.jmix.tool`** ķłź?Code generation tooling:
- `artbuilder/ModuleAlgArtifactGenerator` ķłź?Uses FreeMarker to generate Java constraint code from Module definitions using `ModuleConstraintTemplate.ftl`.
- `bbuilder/ModuleGenneratorByAnno` ķłź?Builds `Module` instances from annotation-decorated classes at runtime.
- `impl/ModuleGenerator` ķłź?LLM-based code generation (via Spring AI).
- `impl/ModulePacker` / `ModuleCompiler` / `ModuleRunner` ķłź?Dynamic compilation and execution pipeline.

### Key Patterns

- **Annotation-driven module definition in tests**: Use `@ModuleAnno`, `@ParaAnno`, `@PartAnno`, `@CodeRuleAnno`, `@CompatiableRuleAnno`, `@PriorityRuleAnno` on a class extending `ConstraintAlgImplTestBase`. These annotations auto-generate the `Module` object.
- **Test base classes**: `ModuleScenarioTestBase` for integration tests (full init ķł½?solve ķł½?assert pipeline), `ConstraintAlgImplTestBase` for constraint algorithm definition.
- **PartCategory** shares the same structure as Module (extends `ModuleBase`), supporting nested constraint hierarchies and multi-instance (MultiInstPartCategory) scenarios.
- **Solver supports**: single solution, all solutions enumeration (`enumerateAllSolution`), priority-layered solving, and conflict detection via relax variables.

## Code Style

- **Log messages MUST be in English** ķłź?this is a hard rule from `.cursorrules`. Use `log.info("User authentication successful")` not `log.info("ķÉóŃä”Õ¤øńÆüŃéłńśēķÄ┤µä¼Õ¦ø")`.
- **Comments may use Chinese** ķłź?JavaDoc and inline comments can use Chinese for explaining complex business logic.
- Communication with this user should be in simplified Chinese.
- Java source level: 21. Max line length: 120 (per `checkstyle.xml` and `CodeFormatterFromIdea.xml`).
- Use Lombok (`@Data`, `@Slf4j`, `@EqualsAndHashCode`) for boilerplate reduction.
```

package com.jmix.tool.impl;

import com.jmix.executor.bmodel.Module;
import com.jmix.executor.impl.util.CommHelper;
import com.jmix.executor.impl.util.ModuleUtils;
import com.jmix.executor.model.AlgLoaderException;
import com.jmix.tool.bbuilder.ModuleGenneratorByAnno;

import lombok.extern.slf4j.Slf4j;

import java.io.File;
import java.io.FileInputStream;
import java.io.FileOutputStream;
import java.io.IOException;
import java.nio.file.Files;
import java.nio.file.Path;
import java.nio.file.Paths;
import java.util.ArrayList;
import java.util.List;
import java.util.jar.JarEntry;
import java.util.jar.JarOutputStream;
import java.util.jar.Manifest;

/**
 * Packs module algorithm artifacts into runtime and source jars.
 *
 * @since 2025-09-27
 */
@Slf4j
public class ModulePacker {

    /**
     * Packs an algorithm class.
     *
     * @param cpClazz    algorithm class
     * @param outRootDir output root directory
     * @return output directory path
     */
    public String pack(Class<?> cpClazz, String outRootDir) {
        Module module = ModuleGenneratorByAnno.buildModule(cpClazz);
        return pack(module, cpClazz, outRootDir);
    }

    /**
     * Packs a module and its algorithm class.
     *
     * @param module     module metadata
     * @param cpClazz    algorithm class
     * @param outRootDir output root directory
     * @return output directory path
     */
    public String pack(Module module, Class<?> cpClazz, String outRootDir) {
        try {
            String inputDir = CommHelper.getJavaFilePath(cpClazz);

            String outDir = module.getAlg().getModuleDirPath(outRootDir);

            Path outDirPath = Paths.get(outDir);
            if (Files.exists(outDirPath)) {
                log.info("Removing existing directory: {}", outDir);
                deleteDirectory(outDirPath);
            }

            Files.createDirectories(outDirPath);
            log.info("Created output directory: {}", outDir);

            String moduleFilePath = module.getAlg().getBaseJsonPath(outRootDir);
            ModuleUtils.toJsonFile(module, moduleFilePath);
            log.info("Module saved to: {}", moduleFilePath);

            boolean isMultifile = isMultifile(cpClazz);
            log.info("Pack mode: {}", isMultifile ? "multifile" : "singlefile");

            boolean classJarSuccess = packClassJar(inputDir, module, isMultifile, outDir);
            if (!classJarSuccess) {
                log.error("Failed to pack class jar");
                throw new AlgLoaderException("Failed to pack class jar");
            }

            boolean sourceJarSuccess = packSourceJar(inputDir, module, isMultifile, outDir);
            if (!sourceJarSuccess) {
                log.error("Failed to pack source jar");
                throw new AlgLoaderException("Failed to pack source jar");
            }

            log.info("Module packed successfully to: {}", outDir);
            return outDir;

        } catch (Exception e) {
            log.error("Failed to pack module", e);
            throw new AlgLoaderException("Failed to pack module: " + e.getMessage(), e);
        }
    }

    /**
     * Returns whether the algorithm is represented by multiple source files.
     *
     * @param cpClassName algorithm class
     * @return true for multifile algorithms, false for single-file tests
     */
    public boolean isMultifile(Class<?> cpClassName) {
        return cpClassName.getEnclosingClass() == null
                && !cpClassName.getSimpleName().endsWith("Test");
    }

    /**
     * Packs class files into the runtime jar.
     *
     * @param inputDir    source input directory
     * @param module      module metadata
     * @param isMultifile whether this is a multifile algorithm
     * @param outDir      output directory
     * @return whether packing succeeded
     */
    public boolean packClassJar(String inputDir, Module module, boolean isMultifile, String outDir) {
        try {
            String jarFilePath = module.getAlg()
                    .getRuntimeJarPath(outDir.substring(0, outDir.lastIndexOf(File.separator)));

            String testClassesDir = inputDir.replace("src\\test\\java", "target\\test-classes");
            log.info("Looking for class files in: {}", testClassesDir);

            List<File> classFiles = new ArrayList<>();

            if (isMultifile) {
                String constraintClassName = module.getCode() + "Constraint";
                String testClassName = module.getCode() + "ConstraintTest";

                findClassFiles(testClassesDir, constraintClassName, classFiles);
                findClassFiles(testClassesDir, testClassName, classFiles);
            } else {
                String testClassName = module.getCode() + "Test";
                String constraintClassName = testClassName + "$" + module.getCode() + "Constraint";

                findClassFiles(tes
... (truncated)

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="exec_command"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **exec_command**</summary>
```bash
$script = @'
from pathlib import Path
import subprocess

bad_markers = [
    'Õ”»','ķÉÄ','ķ¢Ė','ķ¢╣','ķ¢║','ķŚü','Õ©┤','Õ®Ą','ń╗Š','ń╝ü','ķÅē','ńĆĄ','µ┐Ī','ķÅŹ','ńÆü','ķ¢╗','ķłź','ķł¦','ķöø','ķö¤','’┐Į','ķÉĀ','ķ¢╝','µā¦','Õ»«','ń╗ŗ','ķŹ”','ķŹÆ','ķŹÖ','ķŹÅ','ķŹÜ','ķŹō','ķ¬×','µĄŻ','µØ®','µŠČ','ńĆø','ķÅä','µČō','ķĢÉ','µČö','µĄĀ','õĮā','ÕĀó','µ¼Å','Õŗ½','ÕĀ¤','ńŖ│','Õ╝¼','ń«╗','Ķż░','ńåĖ','õĮĖ','µĀ©','Õ×Ü','ńÜæ','ķŚä','Õ×»','Õ¦ś','Õ×░','ń┤Ø','ķŹŖ','ķŠ¤'
]

def bad_score(s: str) -> int:
    return sum(s.count(m) for m in bad_markers)

def replacement_score(s: str) -> int:
    return s.count('\ufffd') + s.count('?')

def try_fix_line(line: str) -> str:
    if bad_score(line) == 0:
        return line
    candidates = []
    for enc in ('gbk', 'cp936'):
        try:
            candidates.append(line.encode(enc).decode('utf-8'))
        except UnicodeError:
            pass
    best = line
    best_tuple = (bad_score(line), replacement_score(line))
    for cand in candidates:
        cand_tuple = (bad_score(cand), replacement_score(cand))
        if cand_tuple < best_tuple:
            best = cand
            best_tuple = cand_tuple
    return best

raw = subprocess.check_output(['git', 'diff', '--name-only', '--', '.', ':(exclude).specstory/**'], text=True)
paths = []
for name in raw.splitlines():
    p = Path(name)
    if not p.exists() or p.is_dir():
        continue
    if p.suffix.lower() in {'.java', '.ftl', '.jtl', '.md', '.xml', '.properties', '.json'} or p.name in {'CLAUDE.md'}:
        paths.append(p)
# Also fix project guidance if present.
if Path('CLAUDE.md').exists() and Path('CLAUDE.md') not in paths:
    paths.append(Path('CLAUDE.md'))

changed = []
for p in paths:
    data = p.read_text(encoding='utf-8', errors='replace')
    fixed_lines = [try_fix_line(line) for line in data.splitlines(keepends=True)]
    fixed = ''.join(fixed_lines)
    if fixed != data:
        p.write_text(fixed, encoding='utf-8', newline='')
        changed.append(str(p))
print('\n'.join(changed))
'@
Set-Content -LiteralPath "$env:TEMP\fix_mojibake.py" -Value $script -Encoding UTF8
python "$env:TEMP\fix_mojibake.py"
```

$script = @'
from pathlib import Path
import subprocess

s = 'Õ”»ŌĆ│µĮĪń╗ĀµźüńĪČńĆ╣ńéĄÕ╣ćń╗½?'
print('decode sample:')
for enc in ['gbk', 'cp936', 'latin1']:
    try:
        print(enc, s.encode(enc).decode('utf-8'))
    except Exception as e:
        print(enc, type(e).__name__, e)

for p in [
    Path('src/main/java/com/jmix/executor/impl/algmodel/ModuleAlgImpl.java'),
    Path('src/main/java/com/jmix/executor/impl/algmodel/ModuleBaseAlgImpl.java'),
    Path('src/main/java/com/jmix/tool/impl/ModulePacker.java'),
    Path('CLAUDE.md')
]:
    print('--- WORKTREE', p)
    text = p.read_text(encoding='utf-8', errors='replace')
    for i, line in enumerate(text.splitlines(), 1):
        if i < 90 and ('*' in line or '//' in line or 'ķłź' in line):
            print(i, repr(line))

for p in [
    'src/main/java/com/jmix/executor/impl/algmodel/ModuleAlgImpl.java',
    'src/main/java/com/jmix/executor/impl/algmodel/ModuleBaseAlgImpl.java',
    'src/main/java/com/jmix/tool/ModelHelper.java',
    'src/main/java/com/jmix/tool/impl/ModulePacker.java',
    'CLAUDE.md'
]:
    print('--- HEAD', p)
    try:
        data = subprocess.check_output(['git', 'show', 'HEAD:' + p])
    except subprocess.CalledProcessError as exc:
        print('missing in HEAD')
        continue
    text = data.decode('utf-8', 'replace')
    for i, line in enumerate(text.splitlines(), 1):
        if i < 90 and ('*' in line or '//' in line or 'ķłź' in line):
            print(i, repr(line))

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="exec_command"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **exec_command**</summary>
```bash
$script = @'
from pathlib import Path
import subprocess

raw = subprocess.check_output(['git', 'diff', '--name-only', '--', '.', ':(exclude).specstory/**'], text=True)
paths = []
for name in raw.splitlines():
    p = Path(name)
    if p.exists() and p.is_file() and p.suffix.lower() in {'.java', '.ftl', '.jtl', '.md', '.xml', '.properties'}:
        paths.append(p)
if Path('CLAUDE.md').exists() and Path('CLAUDE.md') not in paths:
    paths.append(Path('CLAUDE.md'))

bad_tokens = ['’┐Į', '\ue0a2', '\ue1ee', '\ue750', '\ue511', '\ue100', '\ue749', '\ue686', '\ue766', '\ue1ba']
garbled_fragments = ['Õ”»', 'ķÉÄ', 'ķ¢Ė', 'ķ¢╣', 'ķ¢║', 'ķŚü', 'Õ©┤', 'Õ®Ą', 'ń╗Š', 'ń╝ü', 'ķÅē', 'ńĆĄ', 'µ┐Ī', 'ķłź', 'ķł¦', 'ķöø', 'ķÉĀ', 'ķ¢╝']

for p in paths:
    text = p.read_text(encoding='utf-8', errors='replace')
    hits = []
    for i, line in enumerate(text.splitlines(), 1):
        if any(tok in line for tok in bad_tokens) or any(tok in line for tok in garbled_fragments):
            # Prefer actual comments/docs/prompts, but include all for inspection.
            hits.append((i, line))
    if hits:
        print(f'--- {p} ({len(hits)} hits)')
        for i, line in hits[:30]:
            safe = line.encode('unicode_escape').decode('ascii')
            print(f'{i}: {safe}')
        if len(hits) > 30:
            print(f'... {len(hits)-30} more')
'@
Set-Content -LiteralPath "$env:TEMP\list_bad_comments.py" -Value $script -Encoding UTF8
$env:PYTHONIOENCODING='utf-8'
python "$env:TEMP\list_bad_comments.py"
```

    public void init(AlgCPModel model, IModule module,

    protected void executeRuleMethod(Rule rule, Object moduleAlgFile, Method method) {
        if (isOneManyRule(rule)) {
            executeRuleMethod4OneManyRule(rule, moduleAlgFile, method);
        } else {
            super.executeRuleMethod(rule, moduleAlgFile, method);
        }
    }

    private boolean isOneManyRule(Rule rule) {
        return rule.getLeftCardinality() == Cardinality.ONE && rule.getRightCardinality() == Cardinality.MANY;
    }

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="exec_command"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **exec_command**</summary>
```bash
$script = @'
from pathlib import Path
bad_tokens = ['’┐Į']
moji = ['Õ”»','ķÉÄ','ķ¢Ė','ķ¢╣','ķ¢║','ķŚü','Õ©┤','Õ®Ą','ķÅē','ńĆĄ','µ┐Ī','ķłź','ķł¦','ķÉĀ','ķ¢╝','ń╝é','ń╗Š','ń╝ü','ńæ£','Õ®ó','ķ¢╗','ķ¢┐','ķÉ¤','ķÉ×','ķÉÅ','ķÉó','ķÅŗ','ķÅī','ķÅŹ','ķÅä','ķÅé','ķÄĄ','ķÄ╗','ķŹÅ','ķŹÉ','ķŹÆ','ķŹÖ','ķŹÜ','ķŹ”','ķŹ¦','Õ»«','Õ┐ö','µā¦','ÕĀ¤','ńŖ│','õĮĖ','µĀ©','Õ×Ü','ńÜæ','ń╝Ź','ń╗▒','Õ©å','µ╣┤','ńü×','ńåĖ','µ¼Å','Õŗ½','Õ╝¼']
roots = [Path('src/main/java'), Path('src/test/java'), Path('src/main/resources'), Path('CLAUDE.md')]
files = []
for root in roots:
    if root.is_file():
        candidates = [root]
    elif root.exists():
        candidates = [p for p in root.rglob('*') if p.is_file() and p.suffix.lower() in {'.java','.ftl','.jtl','.md','.xml','.properties'}]
    else:
        candidates = []
    for p in candidates:
        text = p.read_text(encoding='utf-8', errors='replace')
        hits = []
        for i, line in enumerate(text.splitlines(), 1):
            if any(t in line for t in bad_tokens) or any(t in line for t in moji):
                hits.append((i,line))
        if hits:
            files.append((p,hits))
for p,hits in files:
    print(f'{p}\t{len(hits)}')
print('TOTAL_FILES', len(files))
print('TOTAL_LINES', sum(len(h) for _,h in files))
'@
Set-Content -LiteralPath "$env:TEMP\scan_moji_files.py" -Value $script -Encoding UTF8
$env:PYTHONIOENCODING='utf-8'
python "$env:TEMP\scan_moji_files.py"
```

    print('SAMPLE', s.encode('unicode_escape').decode())
    for enc in ['gbk','cp936','gb18030']:
        try:
            b=s.encode(enc)
            print(enc, b.hex(), b.decode('utf-8','replace'))
        except Exception as e:
            print(enc, e)
'@
Set-Content -LiteralPath "$env:TEMP\enc_probe.py" -Value $script -Encoding UTF8
$env:PYTHONIOENCODING='utf-8'
python "$env:TEMP\enc_probe.py"
```

```bash
$script = @'
from pathlib import Path
p=Path('src/main/java/com/jmix/executor/impl/algmodel/ModuleAlgImpl.java')
line=p.read_text(encoding='utf-8').splitlines()[46]
print(repr(line), line.encode('unicode_escape').decode())
s=line
for step in range(1,5):
    try:
        s=s.encode('gb18030').decode('utf-8')
    except Exception as e:
        print('step',step,'err',e)
        break
    print('step',step, repr(s), s.encode('unicode_escape').decode())

# transform good line multiple times
s=' * µ©ĪÕØŚń«Śµ│ĢÕ«×ńÄ░ń▒╗'
print('good', s.encode('unicode_escape').decode())
for step in range(1,5):
    s=s.encode('utf-8').decode('gb18030', errors='replace')
    print('moji',step, repr(s), s.encode('unicode_escape').decode())
'@
Set-Content -LiteralPath "$env:TEMP\line_transform.py" -Value $script -Encoding UTF8
$env:PYTHONIOENCODING='utf-8'
python "$env:TEMP\line_transform.py"
```

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="exec_command"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **exec_command**</summary>
```bash
$script = @'
from pathlib import Path
import subprocess

refs = ['HEAD^', '10cdc42', '560fd67', 'c140ce3', '11fc041']
roots = [Path('src/main/java'), Path('src/test/java'), Path('src/main/resources'), Path('CLAUDE.md')]
exts = {'.java', '.ftl', '.jtl', '.md', '.xml', '.properties'}

def git_show(ref, path):
    try:
        return subprocess.check_output(['git', 'show', f'{ref}:{path.as_posix()}'], stderr=subprocess.DEVNULL).decode('utf-8', 'replace')
    except subprocess.CalledProcessError:
        return None

def mojibake(s, passes=1, enc='gb18030', repl='?'):
    out = s
    for _ in range(passes):
        out = out.encode('utf-8').decode(enc, errors='replace')
        if repl is not None:
            out = out.replace('\ufffd', repl)
    return out

def bad(line):
    return any(t in line for t in ['’┐Į','Õ”»','ķÉÄ','ķ¢Ė','ķ¢╣','ķ¢║','ķŚü','Õ©┤','Õ®Ą','ķÅē','ńĆĄ','µ┐Ī','ķłź','ķł¦','ķÉĀ','ķ¢╝','ń╝é','ń╗Š','ń╝ü','ńæ£','Õ®ó','ķ¢╗','ķ¢┐','ķÉ¤','ķÉ×','ķÉÅ','ķÉó','ķÅŗ','ķÅī','ķÅŹ','ķÅä','ķÅé','ķÄĄ','ķÄ╗','ķŹÅ','ķŹÉ','ķŹÆ','ķŹÖ','ķŹÜ','ķŹ”','ķŹ¦','Õ»«','Õ┐ö','µā¦','ÕĀ¤','ńŖ│','õĮĖ','µĀ©','Õ×Ü','ńÜæ','ń╝Ź','ń╗▒','Õ©å','µ╣┤','ńü×','ńåĖ','µ¼Å','Õŗ½','Õ╝¼'])

def is_text_file(p):
    return p.is_file() and (p.suffix.lower() in exts or p.name == 'CLAUDE.md')

paths = []
for root in roots:
    if root.is_file():
        paths.append(root)
    elif root.exists():
        paths.extend(p for p in root.rglob('*') if is_text_file(p))

summary=[]
for p in sorted(set(paths)):
    cur = p.read_text(encoding='utf-8', errors='replace')
    mapping = {}
    for ref in refs:
        ref_text = git_show(ref, p)
        if not ref_text:
            continue
        for line in ref_text.splitlines():
            if not any(ord(ch) > 127 for ch in line):
                continue
            for passes in (1,2):
                for repl in ('?', '\ufffd'):
                    try:
                        m = mojibake(line, passes=passes, repl=repl)
                    except Exception:
                        continue
                    if m != line:
                        mapping.setdefault(m, line)
    hits=0
    for line in cur.splitlines():
        if bad(line) and line in mapping:
            hits += 1
    if hits:
        total_bad = sum(1 for line in cur.splitlines() if bad(line))
        summary.append((str(p), hits, total_bad))
for item in summary:
    print('\t'.join(map(str,item)))
print('FILES', len(summary), 'HITS', sum(x[1] for x in summary), 'BAD', sum(x[2] for x in summary))
'@
Set-Content -LiteralPath "$env:TEMP\dry_restore_comments.py" -Value $script -Encoding UTF8
$env:PYTHONIOENCODING='utf-8'
python "$env:TEMP\dry_restore_comments.py"
```

```bash
$script = @'
from pathlib import Path
import subprocess

refs = ['HEAD^', '10cdc42', '560fd67', 'c140ce3', '11fc041']
roots = [Path('src/main/java'), Path('src/test/java'), Path('src/main/resources'), Path('CLAUDE.md')]
exts = {'.java', '.ftl', '.jtl', '.md', '.xml', '.properties'}

def git_show(ref, path):
    try:
        return subprocess.check_output(['git', 'show', f'{ref}:{path.as_posix()}'], stderr=subprocess.DEVNULL).decode('utf-8', 'replace')
    except subprocess.CalledProcessError:
        return None

def mojibake(s, passes=1, repl='?'):
    out = s
    for _ in range(passes):
        out = out.encode('utf-8').decode('gb18030', errors='replace')
        out = out.replace('\ufffd', repl)
    return out

def bad(line):
    return any(t in line for t in ['’┐Į','Õ”»','ķÉÄ','ķ¢Ė','ķ¢╣','ķ¢║','ķŚü','Õ©┤','Õ®Ą','ķÅē','ńĆĄ','µ┐Ī','ķłź','ķł¦','ķÉĀ','ķ¢╝','ń╝é','ń╗Š','ń╝ü','ńæ£','Õ®ó','ķ¢╗','ķ¢┐','ķÉ¤','ķÉ×','ķÉÅ','ķÉó','ķÅŗ','ķÅī','ķÅŹ','ķÅä','ķÅé','ķÄĄ','ķÄ╗','ķŹÅ','ķŹÉ','ķŹÆ','ķŹÖ','ķŹÜ','ķŹ”','ķŹ¦','Õ»«','Õ┐ö','µā¦','ÕĀ¤','ńŖ│','õĮĖ','µĀ©','Õ×Ü','ńÜæ','ń╝Ź','ń╗▒','Õ©å','µ╣┤','ńü×','ńåĖ','µ¼Å','Õŗ½','Õ╝¼'])

def is_text_file(p):
    return p.is_file() and (p.suffix.lower() in exts or p.name == 'CLAUDE.md')

paths = []
for root in roots:
    if root.is_file():
        paths.append(root)
    elif root.exists():
        paths.extend(p for p in root.rglob('*') if is_text_file(p))

changed=[]
for p in sorted(set(paths)):
    cur = p.read_text(encoding='utf-8', errors='replace')
    mapping = {}
    for ref in refs:
        ref_text = git_show(ref, p)
        if not ref_text:
            continue
        for line in ref_text.splitlines():
            if not any(ord(ch) > 127 for ch in line):
                continue
            for passes in (1,2):
                for repl in ('?', '\ufffd'):
                    m = mojibake(line, passes=passes, repl=repl)
                    if m != line:
                        mapping.setdefault(m, line)
    out=[]
    hits=0
    for line in cur.splitlines(keepends=True):
        body=line[:-1] if line.endswith('\n') else line
        eol='\n' if line.endswith('\n') else ''
        if bad(body) and body in mapping:
            out.append(mapping[body] + eol)
            hits += 1
        else:
            out.append(line)
    if hits:
        p.write_text(''.join(out), encoding='utf-8', newline='')
        changed.append((str(p), hits))
for p,h in changed:
    print(f'{p}\t{h}')
print('FILES', len(changed), 'HITS', sum(h for _,h in changed))
'@

```bash
$script = @'
from pathlib import Path
bad_tokens = ['’┐Į']
moji = ['Õ”»','ķÉÄ','ķ¢Ė','ķ¢╣','ķ¢║','ķŚü','Õ©┤','Õ®Ą','ķÅē','ńĆĄ','µ┐Ī','ķłź','ķł¦','ķÉĀ','ķ¢╝','ń╝é','ń╗Š','ń╝ü','ńæ£','Õ®ó','ķ¢╗','ķ¢┐','ķÉ¤','ķÉ×','ķÉÅ','ķÉó','ķÅŗ','ķÅī','ķÅŹ','ķÅä','ķÅé','ķÄĄ','ķÄ╗','ķŹÅ','ķŹÉ','ķŹÆ','ķŹÖ','ķŹÜ','ķŹ”','ķŹ¦','Õ»«','Õ┐ö','µā¦','ÕĀ¤','ńŖ│','õĮĖ','µĀ©','Õ×Ü','ńÜæ','ń╝Ź','ń╗▒','Õ©å','µ╣┤','ńü×','ńåĖ','µ¼Å','Õŗ½','Õ╝¼']
roots=[Path('src/main/java'),Path('src/test/java'),Path('src/main/resources'),Path('CLAUDE.md')]
exts={'.java','.ftl','.jtl','.md','.xml','.properties'}
files=[]
for root in roots:
    candidates=[root] if root.is_file() else ([p for p in root.rglob('*') if p.is_file() and p.suffix.lower() in exts] if root.exists() else [])
    for p in candidates:
        text=p.read_text(encoding='utf-8',errors='replace')
        hits=[]
        for i,line in enumerate(text.splitlines(),1):
            if any(t in line for t in bad_tokens) or any(t in line for t in moji):
                hits.append((i,line))
        if hits:
            files.append((p,hits))
for p,hits in files:
    print(f'--- {p} ({len(hits)})')
    for i,line in hits[:60]:
        print(f'{i}: {line.encode("unicode_escape").decode("ascii")}')
    if len(hits)>60: print('...')
print('TOTAL_FILES', len(files), 'TOTAL_LINES', sum(len(h) for _,h in files))
'@
Set-Content -LiteralPath "$env:TEMP\remaining_bad.py" -Value $script -Encoding UTF8
$env:PYTHONIOENCODING='utf-8'
python "$env:TEMP\remaining_bad.py"
```

Output:
# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## Build & Test Commands

```bash
# Build (Java 21, Maven 3.6+)
mvn clean compile

# Run all tests
mvn test

# Run a specific test class
mvn test -Dtest=FilterExpressionExecutorTest

# Run a specific test method
mvn test -Dtest=CalculateRuleIfThenTest#testCalculateRule

# Run with exec plugin
mvn exec:java -Dexec.mainClass="com.jmix.configengine.Main"
```

Lombok is used throughout ŌĆö ensure annotation processing is enabled in your IDE. Checkstyle config exists at `checkstyle.xml` but is commented out in `pom.xml`.

## Architecture

This is a **constraint-based configuration engine** that uses Google OR-Tools (CP-SAT solver) to reason about product configurations. The system models products as Modules with Params, Parts, and Rules, then uses the CP-SAT solver to find valid configurations or infer parameter values.

### Layer Model

```
config data (Module/Para/Part/Rule)
       |
       v
Constraint solver (OR-Tools CP-SAT wrapper)
       |
       v
Solution enumeration (ModuleInst/ParaInst/PartInst)
```

### Package Structure

**`com.jmix.executor`** ŌĆö Runtime constraint solving engine:
- `bmodel/` ŌĆö Business domain model: `Module`, `ModuleBase`, `Para`, `Part`, `PartCategory`, `Rule`, and rule schemas (`CompatiableRuleSchema`, `CalculateRuleSchema`, `CodeRuleSchema`, `PriorityRuleSchema`). `ModuleBase` extends `Onto` and serves as the common base for both `Module` and `PartCategory` (they share the same structure of params + parts + rules).
- `cmodel/` ŌĆö Configuration instance model: `ModuleInst`, `ParaInst`, `PartInst`, `SolverResult` ŌĆö these represent solver output, not input.
- `impl/algmodel/` ŌĆö OR-Tools CP-SAT wrapper: `AlgCPModel`, `AlgCPConstraint`, `AlgCPLinearExpr`, plus variable types (`ParaVar`, `PartVar`, `PartCategoryVar`, `RelaxVar`). `ModuleAlgImpl` extends `ModuleBaseAlgImpl` and is the key class that builds CP-SAT constraints from Module data.
- `southinf/` ŌĆö "Southbound interfaces" that generated code implements: `IModuleAlg`, `IConstraintFunction`, `IPartCategoryFunction`.
- `ModuleConstraintExecutor` ŌĆö Singleton entry point (via `INST` field). Only interface external callers should use. Provides `init/fini/addModule/removeModule/inferParas/registerExtensible`.
- `ModuleConstraintExecutorImpl` ŌĆö Implementation with priority-based layered solving, incremental model loading, and conflict detection via relax variables.

**`com.jmix.tool`** ŌĆö Code generation tooling:
- `artbuilder/ModuleAlgArtifactGenerator` ŌĆö Uses FreeMarker to generate Java constraint code from Module definitions using `ModuleConstraintTemplate.ftl`.
- `bbuilder/ModuleGenneratorByAnno` ŌĆö Builds `Module` instances from annotation-decorated classes at runtime.
- `impl/ModuleGenerator` ŌĆö LLM-based code generation (via Spring AI).
- `impl/ModulePacker` / `ModuleCompiler` / `ModuleRunner` ŌĆö Dynamic compilation and execution pipeline.

### Key Patterns

- **Annotation-driven module definition in tests**: Use `@ModuleAnno`, `@ParaAnno`, `@PartAnno`, `@CodeRuleAnno`, `@CompatiableRuleAnno`, `@PriorityRuleAnno` on a class extending `ConstraintAlgImplTestBase`. These annotations auto-generate the `Module` object.
- **Test base classes**: `ModuleScenarioTestBase` for integration tests (full init ŌåÆ solve ŌåÆ assert pipeline), `ConstraintAlgImplTestBase` for constraint algorithm definition.
- **PartCategory** shares the same structure as Module (extends `ModuleBase`), supporting nested constraint hierarchies and multi-instance (MultiInstPartCategory) scenarios.
- **Solver supports**: single solution, all solutions enumeration (`enumerateAllSolution`), priority-layered solving, and conflict detection via relax variables.

## Code Style

- **Log messages MUST be in English** ŌĆö this is a hard rule from `.cursorrules`. Use `log.info("User authentication successful")` not `log.info("ńö©µłĘĶ«żĶ»üµłÉÕŖ¤")`.
- **Comments may use Chinese** ŌĆö JavaDoc and inline comments can use Chinese for explaining complex business logic.
- Communication with this user should be in simplified Chinese.
- Java source level: 21. Max line length: 120 (per `checkstyle.xml` and `CodeFormatterFromIdea.xml`).
- Use Lombok (`@Data`, `@Slf4j`, `@EqualsAndHashCode`) for boilerplate reduction.
```
</details></tool-use>

```bash
@'
from pathlib import Path
import re
pat = re.compile(r'[’┐Įķłźķł¦Õ”»ķÉÄķ¢Ėķ¢╣ķ¢║ķŚüÕ®Ąķöøķ¢┐ķÅēńĆĄķÉĀķ¢╝ń╝éń╗Šń╝üńÆŗńæ£µ┐ĪķÅĆµĄ╝µ©╗ńüēķÄ¼ĶŹżĶó½ķŚéńæÖĶ¦äµĮ»]')
paths=[]
for root in ['src/main/java','src/test/java','src/main/resources']:
    for p in Path(root).rglob('*'):
        if p.is_file():
            paths.append(p)
if Path('CLAUDE.md').exists(): paths.append(Path('CLAUDE.md'))
count=0
for p in paths:
    try: text=p.read_text(encoding='utf-8')
    except UnicodeDecodeError: continue
    for i,line in enumerate(text.splitlines(),1):
        if pat.search(line):
            try:
                dec=line.encode('gbk').decode('utf-8')
            except Exception:
                dec=None
            if dec and dec != line:
                count += 1
                print(f'{p}:{i}')
                print('  OLD:', line)
                print('  NEW:', dec)
                if count >= 120:
                    print('...truncated')
                    raise SystemExit
print('preview_count', count)
'@ | python -

$env:PYTHONIOENCODING='utf-8'; @'
from pathlib import Path
import re, subprocess
bad_pat = re.compile(r'[’┐Įķłźķł¦Õ”»ķÉÄķ¢Ėķ¢╣ķ¢║ķŚüÕ®Ąķöøķ¢┐ķÅēńĆĄķÉĀķ¢╝ń╝éń╗Šń╝üńÆŗÕå¬µĢżµŠČčä─ü]')
# Count conservative obvious bad signatures only, excluding ordinary Chinese as much as possible.
paths=[]
for root in ['src/main/java','src/test/java','src/main/resources']:
    for p in Path(root).rglob('*'):
        if p.is_file(): paths.append(p)
if Path('CLAUDE.md').exists(): paths.append(Path('CLAUDE.md'))
files=[]
for p in paths:
    try: text=p.read_text(encoding='utf-8')
    except UnicodeDecodeError: continue
    lines=[(i,l) for i,l in enumerate(text.splitlines(),1) if bad_pat.search(l)]
    if lines:
        files.append((str(p),len(lines),lines[:10]))
for f,n,sample in files:
    print(f, n)
    for i,l in sample:
        print(f'  {i}: {l}')
print('files', len(files), 'lines', sum(n for _,n,_ in files))
'@ | python -
```

$env:PYTHONIOENCODING='utf-8'; @'
from pathlib import Path

def has_cjk(s):
    return any('\u4e00' <= ch <= '\u9fff' for ch in s)
paths=[]
for root in ['src/main/java','src/test/java','src/main/resources']:
    for p in Path(root).rglob('*'):
        if p.is_file(): paths.append(p)
if Path('CLAUDE.md').exists(): paths.append(Path('CLAUDE.md'))
items=[]
for p in paths:
    try: lines=p.read_text(encoding='utf-8').splitlines()
    except UnicodeDecodeError: continue
    for i,line in enumerate(lines,1):
        try:
            dec=line.encode('gbk').decode('utf-8')
        except UnicodeError:
            continue
        if dec != line and has_cjk(dec):
            items.append((p,i,line,dec))
print('strict_recoverable', len(items))
for p,i,line,dec in items[:120]:
    print(f'{p}:{i}')
    print('OLD_U:', line.encode('unicode_escape').decode())
    print('NEW_U:', dec.encode('unicode_escape').decode())
'@ | python -

    'µ”ø','µ©┐','µ¦ŗ','Ōé¼','ķ¢║','ķ¢Ė','ķÉÄ','ķÉĀ','µ┐Ī','ńĆĄ','ķÅē','ķÉ¤','ń╝ü','ń╗Š','Õ©┤','Õ®Ą',

]
# More precise: line must contain one of mojibake clusters, not just common Chinese chars.
regex = re.compile(r'’┐Į|ķłź|ķł¦|ńÆŗ|Õå¬|µŠČ|ķŹ©|Õ¼Č|ķöø|Õżī|Õ©Ż|ÕŹ×|µ┐Ŗ|ķÅŹĶ¦ä|ķÉóńåĖ|ń╗½ń╗ś|µĄĀµ£Ą|µ”øµ©┐|ķÅŗÕŗ»|ķ¢║|ķ¢Ė|ķÉÄ|ķÉĀ|µ┐ĪÕéø|ńĆĄńæ░|ķÅēŌĢü|ń╝üńŖ│|ń╗ŠŅģ¦|Õ®ĄŌĆ│|ķŹźńāś|ķÉ®µ©╣|µĄ╝µ©║|ńæÖÕŗ½|ķŹÉÕæŁ|µźĀÕ▓ā|ķÅäŅłÜ|µāü|ķŚćŌé¼|ķŹÅńģÄ|’┐Į')
paths=[]
for root in ['src/main/java','src/test/java','src/main/resources']:
    for p in Path(root).rglob('*'):
        if p.is_file(): paths.append(p)
if Path('CLAUDE.md').exists(): paths.append(Path('CLAUDE.md'))
for p in paths:
    try: text=p.read_text(encoding='utf-8')
    except Exception: continue
    hits=[]
    for i,line in enumerate(text.splitlines(),1):
        if regex.search(line): hits.append((i,line))
    if hits:
        print(f'--- {p} ({len(hits)})')

        if len(hits)>50: print('...')

for ref in ['cc1b2a2','10cdc42','560fd67','c140ce3','11fc041']:

        data=subprocess.check_output(['git','show',f'{ref}:{path}'])

    lines=data.decode('utf-8').splitlines()

                print('ERR',e)
    break
'@ | python -

$env:PYTHONIOENCODING='utf-8'; @'
from pathlib import Path
bad_chars=set('’┐Įķłźķł¦Õ”»ķÉÄķ¢Ėķ¢╣ķ¢║ķŚüÕ®Ąķöøķ¢┐ķÅēńĆĄķÉĀķ¢╝ń╝éń╗Šń╝üńÆŗÕå¬µĢżµŠČķŹ©Õ¼ČÕżīµ«æÕ©ŻÕŹ×Õ«│µ┐ŖÕ»«ķÅŹĶ¦äÕĄüķÉóńåĖÕ×Üń╗½µ×āµĄĀµ£Ąń╣ÜńĆøĶż░µÆ│ÕóĀÕ«ĖŃā¦Ō¢╝ńź┤Ķ®”µ”øµ©┐µ¦ŗķŹźķÉ®µ©╣ń┤Ł')
# Print only files touched in current diff and lines containing replacement/private-use or high mojibake markers.
paths=[]
import subprocess
out=subprocess.check_output(['git','diff','--name-only','--','src/main/java','src/test/java','src/main/resources','CLAUDE.md']).decode().splitlines()
for s in out: paths.append(Path(s))
for p in paths:
    if not p.exists(): continue
    lines=p.read_text(encoding='utf-8').splitlines()
    hits=[]
    for i,l in enumerate(lines,1):
        if '\ufffd' in l or any(0xE000 <= ord(ch) <= 0xF8FF for ch in l) or sum(ch in bad_chars for ch in l)>=2:
            hits.append((i,l))
    if hits:
        print(f'--- {p} {len(hits)}')
        for i,l in hits[:80]: print(f'{i}: {l.encode("unicode_escape").decode()}')
        if len(hits)>80: print('...')
'@ | python -
```

```bash
$env:PYTHONIOENCODING='utf-8'; @'
from pathlib import Path
import subprocess, re
# Strong mojibake signatures. Keep this conservative to avoid touching normal Chinese.
bad_re = re.compile(r'[\ufffd\ue000-\uf8ff]|ķłź|ķł¦|ńÆŗ|Õå¬|µŠČ|ķŹ©|Õ¼Č|ķöø|Õżī|Õ©Ż|ÕŹ×|µ┐Ŗ|ķÅŹĶ¦ä|ķÉóńåĖ|ń╗½ń╗ś|µĄĀµ£Ą|µ”øµ©┐|ķÅŗÕŗ»|ķ¢║|ķ¢Ė|ķÉÄ|ķÉĀ|µ┐ĪÕéø|ńĆĄńæ░|ķÅēŌĢü|ń╝üńŖ│|ń╗Š\ue5cb|Õ®Ą|ķŹźńāś|ķÉ®µ©╣|µĄ╝µ©║|ńæÖÕŗ½|ķŹÉÕæŁ|µźĀÕ▓ā|ķŚćŌé¼|ķŹÅńģÄ|ķÉ©Õŗ¼|ķÅäŅłÜ|µāü|Õ”½Ōé¼|µĢ×ķŹÅ|ķÅĆńæ░|ńæÖ’Įł|Õ©┤Õ¼Ł|ķÅŗÕŗ»|ķæŠÕ│░|ńÆüÕē¦|µŠČÕŗŁ|ń║ŁŅå╗|ńü×ńé┤|ń╗½Ķ»▓|ķŹÖÕéø|ńĆ╣ńé░|ķÉóŃäż|ķŹöńŖ║|ńÆćĶ»▓')
# Badness score counts replacement/private chars and common mojibake markers.
def bad_score(s: str) -> int:
    score = 0
    for ch in s:
        o = ord(ch)
        if ch == '\ufffd' or ch == '?' or 0xE000 <= o <= 0xF8FF:
            score += 5
    markers = ['ķłź','ķł¦','ńÆŗ','Õå¬','µŠČ','ķŹ©','Õ¼Č','ķöø','Õżī','Õ©Ż','ÕŹ×','µ┐Ŗ','ķÅŹĶ¦ä','ķÉóńåĖ','ń╗½ń╗ś','µĄĀµ£Ą','µ”øµ©┐','ķÅŗÕŗ»','ķ¢║','ķ¢Ė','ķÉÄ','ķÉĀ','µ┐ĪÕéø','ńĆĄńæ░','ķÅēŌĢü','ń╝üńŖ│','ń╗Š','Õ®Ą','ķŹźńāś','ķÉ®µ©╣','µĄ╝µ©║','ńæÖÕŗ½','ķŹÉÕæŁ','µźĀÕ▓ā','ķŚćŌé¼','ķŹÅńģÄ','Õ”½Ōé¼','ķÅĆńæ░','ķæŠÕ│░','ńÆüÕē¦','µŠČÕŗŁ','ńü×ńé┤','ń╗½Ķ»▓','ķŹÖÕéø','ńĆ╣ńé░','ķÉóŃäż','ķŹöńŖ║','ńÆćĶ»▓']
    score += sum(s.count(m) * 3 for m in markers)
    return score

def cjk_count(s: str) -> int:
    return sum('\u4e00' <= ch <= '\u9fff' for ch in s)

names = subprocess.check_output(['git','diff','--name-only','--','src/main/java','src/test/java','src/main/resources','CLAUDE.md'], text=True).splitlines()
changed = []
for name in names:
    p = Path(name)
    if not p.exists() or not p.is_file():
        continue
    try:
        text = p.read_text(encoding='utf-8')
    except UnicodeDecodeError:
        continue
    lines = text.splitlines(keepends=True)
    new_lines = []
    n_changes = 0
    for line in lines:
        body = line[:-2] if line.endswith('\r\n') else line[:-1] if line.endswith('\n') else line
        eol = line[len(body):]
        if bad_re.search(body):
            best = body
            best_score = bad_score(body)
            for enc in ('gbk', 'gb18030'):
                for err in ('strict', 'replace'):
                    try:
                        cand = body.encode(enc, errors=err).decode('utf-8', errors='replace')
                    except UnicodeError:
                        continue
                    # Keep only clear improvements. Prefer more real CJK and lower mojibake score.
                    cand_score = bad_score(cand)
                    if cand_score + 2 < best_score and cjk_count(cand) >= max(1, cjk_count(body) // 3):
                        best, best_score = cand, cand_score
            if best != body:
                body = best
                n_changes += 1
        new_lines.append(body + eol)
    if n_changes:
        p.write_text(''.join(new_lines), encoding='utf-8', newline='')
        changed.append((name, n_changes))
for name, n in changed:
    print(f'{name}: {n}')
print('files_changed', len(changed), 'lines_changed', sum(n for _, n in changed))
'@ | python -
```

from pathlib import Path
import subprocess
markers = ['ķłź','ķł¦','ńÆŗ','Õå¬','µŠČ','ķŹ©','Õ¼Č','ķöø','Õżī','Õ©Ż','ÕŹ×','µ┐Ŗ','ķÅŹĶ¦ä','ķÉóńåĖ','ń╗½ń╗ś','µĄĀµ£Ą','µ”øµ©┐','ķÅŗÕŗ»','ķ¢║','ķ¢Ė','ķÉÄ','ķÉĀ','µ┐ĪÕéø','ńĆĄńæ░','ķÅēŌĢü','ń╝üńŖ│','ń╗Š','Õ®Ą','ķŹźńāś','ķÉ®µ©╣','µĄ╝µ©║','ńæÖÕŗ½','ķŹÉÕæŁ','µźĀÕ▓ā','ķŚćŌé¼','ķŹÅńģÄ','Õ”½Ōé¼','ķÅĆńæ░','ķæŠÕ│░','ńÆüÕē¦','µŠČÕŗŁ','ńü×ńé┤','ń╗½Ķ»▓','ķŹÖÕéø','ńĆ╣ńé░','ķÉóŃäż','ķŹöńŖ║','ńÆćĶ»▓','µĢ×ķŹÅ','ķÉ©Õŗ¼','ķÅäŅłÜ','µāü']

def has_bad(s):
    return any(ch == '\ufffd' or 0xE000 <= ord(ch) <= 0xF8FF for ch in s) or any(m in s for m in markers)

def bad_score(s):
    score = 0
    for ch in s:
        o = ord(ch)
        if ch == '\ufffd' or ch == '?' or 0xE000 <= o <= 0xF8FF:
            score += 5
    score += sum(s.count(m) * 3 for m in markers)
    return score

def cjk_count(s):
    return sum('\u4e00' <= ch <= '\u9fff' for ch in s)

names = subprocess.check_output(['git','diff','--name-only','--','src/main/java','src/test/java','src/main/resources','CLAUDE.md'], text=True).splitlines()
changed=[]
for name in names:
    p=Path(name)
    if not p.exists() or not p.is_file(): continue
    try: text=p.read_text(encoding='utf-8')
    except UnicodeDecodeError: continue
    lines=text.splitlines(keepends=True)
    out=[]; n=0
    for line in lines:
        if line.endswith('\r\n'):
            body,eol=line[:-2],'\r\n'
        elif line.endswith('\n'):
            body,eol=line[:-1],'\n'
        else:
            body,eol=line,''
        if has_bad(body):
            best=body; best_score=bad_score(body)
            for enc in ('gbk','gb18030'):
                for err in ('strict','replace'):
                    try:
                        cand=body.encode(enc, errors=err).decode('utf-8', errors='replace')
                    except UnicodeError:

                    cand_score=bad_score(cand)
                    if cand_score + 2 < best_score and cjk_count(cand) >= max(1, cjk_count(body)//4):
                        best, best_score = cand, cand_score
            if best != body:
                body=best; n+=1
        out.append(body+eol)
    if n:
        p.write_text(''.join(out), encoding='utf-8', newline='')
        changed.append((name,n))
for name,n in changed:
    print(f'{name}: {n}')
print('files_changed', len(changed), 'lines_changed', sum(n for _,n in changed))
'@ | python -
```

```bash
$env:PYTHONIOENCODING='utf-8'; @'
import subprocess
from pathlib import Path
refs=['HEAD','cc1b2a2','10cdc42','560fd67','533d4abf1318a42914f69f336a537448d6b15cf4','15911f8','4f40394','97a1c73']
# current target files from current diff plus any currently bad under src
names=set(subprocess.check_output(['git','diff','--name-only','--','src/main/java','src/test/java','src/main/resources','CLAUDE.md'], text=True).splitlines())
for root in ['src/main/java','src/test/java','src/main/resources']:
    for p in Path(root).rglob('*'):
        if p.is_file(): names.add(str(p).replace('\\','/'))
if Path('CLAUDE.md').exists(): names.add('CLAUDE.md')
markers=['’┐Į','ķłź','ķł¦','ńÆŗ','Õå¬','µŠČ','ķŹ©','Õ¼Č','ķöø','Õżī','Õ©Ż','ÕŹ×','µ┐Ŗ','ķÅŹĶ¦ä','ķÉóńåĖ','ń╗½ń╗ś','µĄĀµ£Ą','µ”øµ©┐','ķÅŗÕŗ»','ķ¢║','ķ¢Ė','ķÉÄ','ķÉĀ','µ┐ĪÕéø','ńĆĄńæ░','ķÅēŌĢü','ń╝üńŖ│','ń╗Š\ue5cb','Õ®Ą','ķŹźńāś','ķÉ®µ©╣','µĄ╝µ©║','ńæÖÕŗ½','ķŹÉÕæŁ','µźĀÕ▓ā','ķŚćŌé¼','ķŹÅńģÄ','Õ”½Ōé¼','ķÅĆńæ░','ķæŠÕ│░','ńÆüÕē¦','µŠČÕŗŁ','ńü×ńé┤','ń╗½Ķ»▓','ķŹÖÕéø','ńĆ╣ńé░','ķÉóŃäż','ķŹöńŖ║','ńÆćĶ»▓','µĢ×ķŹÅ','ķÉ©Õŗ¼','ķÅäŅłÜ','µāü']
def bad_line(l):
    return any(ch=='\ufffd' or 0xE000 <= ord(ch) <= 0xF8FF for ch in l) or any(m in l for m in markers)
for ref in refs:
    files=0; lines=0; missing=0
    for name in names:
        try:
            data=subprocess.check_output(['git','show',f'{ref}:{name}'], stderr=subprocess.DEVNULL)
        except subprocess.CalledProcessError:
            missing+=1; continue
        try: text=data.decode('utf-8')
        except UnicodeDecodeError:
            continue
        n=sum(1 for l in text.splitlines() if bad_line(l))
        if n:
            files+=1; lines+=n
    print(f'{ref}: bad_files={files} bad_lines={lines} missing={missing}')
'@ | python -

markers=['’┐Į','ķłź','ķł¦','ńÆŗ','Õå¬','µŠČ','ķŹ©','Õ¼Č','ķöø','Õżī','Õ©Ż','ÕŹ×','µ┐Ŗ','ķÅŹĶ¦ä','ķÉóńåĖ','ń╗½ń╗ś','µĄĀµ£Ą','µ”øµ©┐','ķÅŗÕŗ»','ķ¢║','ķ¢Ė','ķÉÄ','ķÉĀ','µ┐ĪÕéø','ńĆĄńæ░','ķÅēŌĢü','ń╝üńŖ│','ń╗Š','Õ®Ą','ķŹźńāś','ķÉ®µ©╣','µĄ╝µ©║','ńæÖÕŗ½','ķŹÉÕæŁ','µźĀÕ▓ā','ķŚćŌé¼','ķŹÅńģÄ','Õ”½Ōé¼','ķÅĆńæ░','ķæŠÕ│░','ńÆüÕē¦','µŠČÕŗŁ','ńü×ńé┤','ń╗½Ķ»▓','ķŹÖÕéø','ńĆ╣ńé░','ķÉóŃäż','ķŹöńŖ║','ńÆćĶ»▓','µĢ×ķŹÅ','ķÉ©Õŗ¼','ķÅäŅłÜ','µāü','ķēü']
def has_bad(s):
    return any(ch == '\ufffd' or 0xE000 <= ord(ch) <= 0xF8FF for ch in s) or any(m in s for m in markers)
def is_commentish(s):
    t=s.strip()
    return t.startswith('//') or t.startswith('*') or t.startswith('/**') or 'normalNaturalCode' in t or 'description =' in t or 'log.info' in t or 'log.warn' in t or 'log.error' in t
hist={}
for r in refs:
    hist[r]={}
    for f in files:
        try:
            hist[r][f]=subprocess.check_output(['git','show',f'{r}:{f}'], stderr=subprocess.DEVNULL).decode('utf-8').splitlines()
        except Exception:
            hist[r][f]=None
for f in files:
    p=Path(f)
    if not p.exists(): continue
    lines=p.read_text(encoding='utf-8').splitlines()
    hits=[]
    for i,l in enumerate(lines,1):
        if has_bad(l) and is_commentish(l):
            cands=[]
            for r in refs:
                h=hist[r].get(f)
                if h and i<=len(h):
                    hl=h[i-1]
                    if not has_bad(hl):
                        cands.append((r,hl))
            hits.append((i,l,cands[:2]))
    if hits:
        print('====',f,'hits',len(hits))
        for i,l,cands in hits[:120]:
            print(f'{i}: CUR: {l}')
            for r,hl in cands:
                print(f'   {r}: {hl}')
            if not cands: print('   NO_CAND')
        if len(hits)>120: print('...')
'@ | python -
